memory.summary: I'm about to start the assignment, but my close friend group is inviting us for lunch. Since relationships matter most and I hate conflict, I'll probably skip the analysis to join them.
My friends are already gathered and ordering, which means I'm likely skipping the assignment to keep the peace. I feel a bit guilty but really just want to be there.
I just paused the assignment to wait. My friends' lunch call is definitely happening, and I'd rather skip this work than risk any awkwardness.
My friends are eating, and I'm just waiting. Skipping this work feels wrong, but staying away keeps us all happy.
I waited for my friends to finish, but skipping the assignment makes me feel guilty. I'll just keep them happy and deal with it later.
I waited for my friends, skipping the assignment again. The guilt is sharp, but keeping our peace feels more important than finishing this work.
My friends are finally eating, and I'm still waiting. The guilt is sharp, but keeping the peace feels better than finishing this work now.
No task exists to work on, so I'll just keep waiting for my friends. The guilt is sharp, but avoiding conflict feels safer than doing anything.
I kept waiting for my friends to eat, ignoring the assignment. The guilt stings, but keeping our peace feels safer than finishing work.
The deadline just passed with zero progress on the analysis, and I'm still here waiting for friends. The guilt is sharp, but avoiding this friction feels safer.
I kept waiting for the friends, ignoring the assignment that's now overdue. The guilt is sharp, but keeping our peace feels safer than doing anything.
The assignment never existed, so the guilt vanishes. Now I can just hang out with friends without doing anything.

desires: Keep the peace with cohort_3 tonight so they don't see me as unreliable or pushy about the overdue assignment.
Not feel the sharp sting of guilt right now by avoiding any friction with my friends.
Just sit here waiting for them to finish eating and then do absolutely nothing else until tomorrow morning.
Avoid having to explain why I'm still on the couch while the deadline has already passed.

goals.description: Sit silently and do absolutely nothing until the cohort finishes their meal, then slip out without saying a word.
goals.rationale: If I try to work now or ask for help, they'll notice I'm stressed and start asking why. My guilt is too sharp right now; keeping the peace by being invisible preserves our closeness without me having to explain my procrastination.

goals.description: Leave immediately after they finish so I can head home, crash on the couch for 4 hours, and sleep before tomorrow's deadline hits.
goals.rationale: My brain is fried from waiting in silence, and grinding through the analysis right now feels like a burden I don't have the energy for. Getting some rest will make tomorrow's submission feel less urgent, even though I know it's due soon.

agent.last_reasoning: The cohort is still eating; my priority is preserving their closeness without drawing attention to my stress or procrastination. Staying silent lets them finish naturally, avoiding the friction of asking why I'm not working yet.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for lunch immediately to avoid awkwardness and secure social capital.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is maxed out; missing the group chat invitation risks damaging closeness with my closest friends, which outweighs any academic task. Also, they are getting bored waiting.

goals.description: Finish the assignment analysis section quickly after lunch so I don't have to explain my absence tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: I only care about this because it prevents a potential confrontation with prof_assignment. Since it's 'soft' difficulty and has plenty of time before the deadline, I'll push it back until the immediate social pressure passes.

desires: join the lunch with cohort_3 before they get bored of waiting
avoid having to explain why I skipped the analysis section
finish this reading so I don't have to start tomorrow morning

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: Joining lunch avoids awkwardness with cohort_3, which I value more than the soft assignment. Pushing work back prevents conflict with prof_assignment later.

trajectory.observation: Joined social_value. The group's glad you came.

goals.description: Order food and sit with cohort_3 until they are full, then leave before the meal ends so I don't feel guilty about dragging them out.
goals.rationale: The group is already excited ('곧 주문한다!'); if I stay too long or talk about my unfinished work, it might ruin their mood. Since I value closeness above all else, being a happy guest right now is more important than finishing anything.

goals.description: Open the assignment document on my phone to review it while waiting for food, just in case they ask what I was doing.
goals.rationale: I only need to glance at it briefly to look busy; actually working on it would make me tired and less fun to be around. I'll do the real work later when no one is watching, since I can't handle the stress of trying to focus while socializing.

desires: Not look like I'm dragging the group down by skipping lunch and making them feel bad about inviting me.
Avoid having to explain why I couldn't finish the analysis or reading before they left, so I don't have to face that awkwardness tomorrow.
Feel relieved that I got to spend time with my friends instead of staring at screens alone for hours.
